en.extract:
The green broadbill (Calyptomena viridis) also known as the lesser green broadbill is a small bird in the family Calyptomenidae. It was formerly classified in the family Eurylaimidae, a group of closely related birds that share the name "broadbill".


Taxonomy
There are three subspecies recognized:

Calyptomena viridis viridis Raffles, 1822 - the Malay Peninsula, extirpated from Singapore
Calyptomena viridis gloriosa Deignan, 1947 - Sumatra, Borneo, Nias, the Lingga Islands, and the Natuna Islands
Calyptomena viridis siberu Chasen & Kloss, 1926 - Mentawai Islands
Prior to revisions by the International Ornithological Congress in 2022, the mainland subspecies went by the name caudacuta, while viridis referred to the Greater Sundaic subspecies. Based on several previous studies finding problems with this nomenclature, the International Ornithological Congress replaced caudacuta with viridis, while gloriosa replaced the former viridis.


Distribution and habitat
The green broadbill is distributed in broadleaved evergreen forests of Borneo, Sumatra, and the Malay Peninsula where it frequents lowland and lower montane rainforest.


Description
The bird is about 17 cm long, plumaged in brilliant green with a black ear patch, widely gaped bill, rounded head, short tail and three black bars on each wing. The beak itself is very weak and almost hidden by the crest above it. Both sexes are similar. The female is duller and has no black markings on its ear patch and wing coverts.


Behaviour
It is often overlooked, as it sits motionless inside the canopy or just below, quickly flying to a new location if disturbed. Its foliage-green color provides excellent camouflage.
It feeds largely on soft figs. The broadbill's feeding habits helps to distribute the seeds of the fig around the forest floor. The female usually lays between two and three whitish eggs, and the young fledge after twenty-two to twenty-three days.


Status and conservation
Although this species remains common in many parts of its large range, it is considered Near Threatened as it is sensitive to habitat loss and degradation, which are likely to be causing population declines throughout its range.
Due to continuing habitat loss, the green broadbill is evaluated as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species.


References

strange, Morten (2000). A Photographic Guide to the Birds of Thailand Including Southeast Asia & The Philippines (1st ed.). Eric Oey (Periplus). ISBN 978-962-593-926-1.
perrins, Christopher M. (2003). Firefly Encyclopedia of Birds (1st ed.). Firefly Books, Ltd. ISBN 1-55297-777-3.


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet Archived 2007-09-29 at the Wayback Machine
Red Data Book

[[File:Calyptomena viridis 82277089.jpg|thumb|Nest]]

The '''green broadbill''' ('''''Calyptomena viridis''''') also known as the '''lesser green broadbill''' is a small bird in the [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Calyptomenidae]]. It was formerly classified in the family [[Eurylaimidae]], a group of closely related birds that share the name "broadbill".

== Taxonomy ==
There are three subspecies recognized:<ref>{{Cite web |title=NZ wrens, Sapayoa, asities, broadbills, pittas – IOC World Bird List |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/nz_wrens/ |access-date=2022-06-10 |language=en-US}}</ref>

* ''Calyptomena viridis viridis'' [[Stamford Raffles|Raffles]], 1822 - the [[Malay Peninsula]], [[Local extinction|extirpated]] from [[Singapore]]
* ''Calyptomena viridis gloriosa'' [[Herbert Girton Deignan|Deignan]], 1947 - [[Sumatra]], [[Borneo]], [[Nias]], the [[Lingga Regency|Lingga Islands]], and the [[Natuna Regency|Natuna Islands]]
* ''Calyptomena viridis siberu'' [[Frederick Nutter Chasen|Chasen]] & [[C. Boden Kloss|Kloss]], 1926 - [[Mentawai Islands Regency|Mentawai Islands]]

Prior to revisions by the [[International Ornithologists' Union|International Ornithological Congress]] in 2022, the mainland subspecies went by the name ''caudacuta'', while ''viridis'' referred to the [[Greater Sunda Islands|Greater Sundaic]] subspecies. Based on several previous studies finding problems with this nomenclature, the International Ornithological Congress replaced ''caudacuta'' with ''viridis'', while ''gloriosa'' replaced the former ''viridis''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Subspecies Updates – IOC World Bird List |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/subspecies-2/ |access-date=2022-06-10 |language=en-US}}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
The green broadbill is distributed in broadleaved evergreen forests of [[Borneo]], [[Sumatra]], and the [[Malay Peninsula]] where it frequents lowland and lower [[Montane forest|montane]] rainforest.

==Description==
The bird is about 17 cm long, plumaged in brilliant green with a black ear patch, widely gaped bill, rounded head, short tail and three black bars on each wing. The [[Bird anatomy|beak]] itself is very weak and almost hidden by the crest above it. Both sexes are similar. The female is duller and has no black markings on its ear patch and [[Bird anatomy|wing coverts]].

==Behaviour==
It is often overlooked, as it sits motionless inside the [[Canopy (biology)|canopy]] or just below, quickly flying to a new location if disturbed. Its foliage-green color provides excellent camouflage.

It feeds largely on soft [[ficus|fig]]s. The broadbill's feeding habits helps to distribute the seeds of the fig around the forest floor. The female usually lays between two and three whitish eggs, and the young fledge after twenty-two to twenty-three days.

==Status and conservation==
Although this species remains common in many parts of its large range, it is considered Near Threatened as it is sensitive to habitat loss and degradation, which are likely to be causing population declines throughout its range.
Due to continuing habitat loss, the green broadbill is evaluated as Near Threatened on the [[IUCN Red List]] of Threatened Species.<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

zh.extract:
绿阔嘴鸟（学名：Calyptomena viridis），是阔嘴鸟科绿阔嘴鸟属的一种，分布于缅甸、文莱（已绝灭）、新加坡、泰国、印度尼西亚和马来西亚。全球活动范围约为2,910,000平方千米。该物种的保护状况被评为近危。
其种加词“viridis”意为“绿色的”。
绿阔嘴鸟的平均体重约为54.2克。栖息地包括种植园、亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、亚热带或热带的沼泽林和亚热带或热带的湿润山地林。


参考文献

Calyptomena viridis. BirdLife.org.   [2011-06-27]. （原始内容存档于2016-03-04）.
